meta.title: BMW N47 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW N47 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW N47 is a 2.0-litre turbodiesel four — reliable overall, but its rear‑mounted timing chain is this engine's defining risk. Backed by 1,450+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

rows.value: H2: BMW N47 — Our Verdict & Rating
Confidence: 92% confidence — based on 1,450 UK enquiries and cross‑referenced specialist rebuild reports [BMW-VERIFIED].
Midpoint of Reconditioned supply range (£3,200–£5,500 → £4,350) — £3,000–£4,999 bracket → 16

rows.metric: Mix of cheap fixes (EGR, sensors) and a few expensive ones (timing chain, turbo)

rows.value: Parts are cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers same‑day; N47 is one of the most common BMW diesels

rows.value: One-line verdict: "The N47 is dependable long‑term — but its rear‑mounted timing chain fails without warning past 100,000 miles — buy only with documented chain history." (Clause 1: 6 words. Clause 2: 11 words. Clause 3: 6 words. Total: 23 words.)

rows.metric: Best For: High‑mileage motorway drivers who service on schedule.
rows.value: Avoid If: No documented timing‑chain history above 80,000 miles.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW N47 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE].

cta.label: → Compare BMW N47 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does a BMW N47 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned N47 typically costs £3,200–£5,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], varying by displacement and specific sub‑variant. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the BMW N47 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The N47 scores 3½★ out of 5 with 92% confidence [BMW-VERIFIED]. It's generally dependable but the rear‑mounted timing chain is a known weakness that can fail without warning, especially past 100,000 miles.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common BMW N47 failure?"
items.answer: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure — the rear‑mounted design makes early detection difficult. Cold‑start rattle is the early warning. Typically occurs 60,000–120,000 miles [PATTERN DATA].

items.question: Fitment — "Which BMW models use the N47?"
items.answer: The N47 powered the 1 Series (E81/F20), 3 Series (E90/F30), 5 Series (F10 520d), X1 (E84), X3 (F25) and X5 (F15 25d) across 2007–2019, making it BMW's most ubiquitous modern diesel.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the BMW N47 last?"
items.answer: With preventative timing‑chain replacement at ~80,000 miles and regular oil changes, the N47 can exceed 200,000 miles. Ignoring chain wear can cause catastrophic engine failure, drastically shortening life.
